Course Title: Who Is Liable for AI-Driven Advice?
Category: Finance & Professional Services: Banking, Insurance, Accounting, Consulting, Legal, Real Estate
Who is this for: Financial advisors, insurance agents, accountants, lawyers, consultants, compliance officers, risk managers, client-facing professionals, business analysts, relationship managers
Team or Dept this is for: Legal, Compliance, Risk Management, Professional Services, Finance, Operations, Client Services
Description: Legal and professional accountability in AI-assisted decisions.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how professional services such as banking, insurance, legal, or accounting operate in practice is helpful, though no prior knowledge of artificial intelligence or law is required.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key legal frameworks governing liability in AI-assisted financial and professional advice, and explain how accountability is allocated between firms, practitioners, and technology providers. You will also apply practical strategies to assess and manage professional risk when AI tools are used to support client-facing decisions in sectors such as banking, insurance, legal services, and consulting.
